_comment: Add the 'testnux' block to the 'mcpServers' object in your Claude Code settings.
_instructions: 1. Open Claude Code settings: Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P → 'Open Settings (JSON)'
2. Add the 'testnux' entry inside the 'mcpServers' object (create it if absent).
3. Save the file and restart Claude Code.
4. Verify: Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P → 'MCP: List Servers' — testnux should appear.
Option A — use npx (no global install required, always fetches latest):
Option B — use a local checkout (for contributors or offline use):
Replace <REPO_PATH> with the absolute path to your testnux clone.

testnux._description: Option A: npx — fetches testnux from npm, boots the MCP server. No global install required.

testnux-local._description: Option B: local checkout — replace <REPO_PATH> with the absolute path to your testnux repo.
